name: Sierra de la Grazalema - from Cortes de la Frontera to Ubrique – circular
name_original: Sierra de la Grazalema - de Cortes de la Frontera a Ubrique – circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Río Guadiaro are characterized by diverse and scenic landscapes within the Serranía de Ronda and Grazalema Natural Park. The region offers undulating mountain loops with significant elevation gains, featuring long ascents and descents. Cyclists encounter well-surfaced asphalt roads with minimal traffic, winding through cork oak groves, laurel forests, and the geological formations of the Guadiaro Valley. This terrain provides varied challenges for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Río Guadiaro The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de la Grazalema - from Cortes de la Frontera to Ubrique – circular, a 40.8 miles (65.7 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 32 minutes to complete. This route features substantial elevation gain through the Sierra de Grazalema. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of Casares – Peñas Blancas Ascent loop from Cortes de la Frontera, a difficult 77.9 miles (125.4 km) path. This route includes a significant ascent and offers expansive views of the surrounding landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Ronda City Walls – Puerto de Encinas Borrachas loop from Cortes de la Frontera, a 39.3 miles (63.2 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Road cycling around Río Guadiaro is defined by undulating mountain loops, quiet asphalt roads, and diverse ecosystems including cork oak and laurel forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, predominantly featuring difficult routes. The routes in Río Guadiaro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1,400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Río Guadiaro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Río Guadiaro region?
A: The Río Guadiaro region offers a diverse selection of over 20 road cycling routes. These routes primarily feature challenging terrain, with 18 classified as difficult and 4 as moderate, catering to cyclists seeking significant elevation gains and varied landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Río Guadiaro?
A: Road cycling in Río Guadiaro is characterized by undulating mountain loops with long ascents and descents, often featuring significant elevation gains, sometimes around 1,000 meters for every 50 kilometers. The region boasts well-surfaced asphalt roads with minimal traffic, winding through diverse ecosystems like cork oak groves, laurel forests, and the unique geological formations of the Guadiaro Valley.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Río Guadiaro?
A: While the Río Guadiaro region is known for its challenging terrain, with most routes classified as difficult, there are a few moderate options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38165557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Mojón de la Víbora loop from Residencial La Palacia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 33 km route with over 500 meters of elevation gain, offering a less strenuous introduction to the area's beauty.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass through the stunning Serranía de Ronda and Grazalema Natural Park, offering views of diverse ecosystems. You can expect to see cork oak groves, laurel forests, and unique alder groves along the rivers. The region also features impressive karst landscapes and the spectacular limestone gorge of the Angosturas del Río Guadiaro between Benaoján and Jimera de Líbar. Many routes also wind through charming White Villages (Pueblos Blancos).

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, the Río Guadiaro region offers several long-distance routes for experienced road cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33886449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Casares – Peñas Blancas Ascent loop from Cortes de la Frontera&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 125 km path with over 3,300 meters of ascent. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40018793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Mojón de la Víbora – Picao Bridge loop from Cortes de la Frontera&lt;/a&gt;, spanning over 154 km with significant elevation.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Río Guadiaro?
A: The road cycling routes in Río Guadiaro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 18 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the quality and condition of the roads, the minimal traffic, and the breathtaking scenery through the undulating mountain loops and diverse natural parks.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in the area?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Río Guadiaro are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977065517/sierra-de-la-grazalema-from-cortes-de-la-frontera-to-ubrique-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Grazalema - from Cortes de la Frontera to Ubrique – circular&lt;/a&gt;, a 65.7 km route through the Sierra de Grazalema.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Río Guadiaro?
A: The region's climate generally makes it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for tackling the significant elevation gains. Summers can be hot, especially in the valleys, while winters are milder but can bring cooler temperatures to higher elevations.

Q: Can I find cafes, pubs, or accommodation along the road cycling routes?
A: Many road cycling routes in Río Guadiaro pass through or near charming White Villages (Pueblos Blancos). These villages often provide opportunities for scenic stops where you can find local cafes, pubs, and various accommodation options, allowing for cultural immersion and refreshment during your ride.

Q: Are there any specific road cycling routes that offer particularly scenic viewpoints?
A: The entire region is renowned for its stunning views. Routes that traverse the Serranía de Ronda and Grazalema Natural Park, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40019452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pass of the Doves – Grazalema Village loop from Jimera de Líbar&lt;/a&gt;, offer expansive vistas of diverse ecosystems and mountainous terrain. The Angosturas del Río Guadiaro also provides dramatic limestone gorge views.

Q: Is the Estuario del Río Guadiaro Natural Area suitable for road cycling?
A: The Estuario del Río Guadiaro Natural Area, located at the river's mouth, offers a different kind of natural beauty, particularly for birdwatching. While the broader region is known for challenging road cycling, this specific area features easier, mostly paved routes, making it suitable for a more relaxed ride. For more information on this natural area, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.visit-andalucia.com/national_park_andalucia.php?park=estuario-del-rio-guadiaro-paraje-natural" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visit-andalucia.com&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How accessible is the Río Guadiaro region for cyclists using public transport?
A: While specific public transport options directly to trailheads can vary, the region is part of the wider Serranía de Ronda, which has some train and bus connections to larger towns. Cyclists often travel to key starting points like Cortes de la Frontera or Jimera de Líbar, which serve as excellent bases for exploring the surrounding road cycling network.
tips: A lonely road in spring (beginning of May) that leads through the southern part of the Sierra Grazalema Natural Park to the junction to Ubrique and guarantees great views. A must for racing cyclists.
Right next to this mountain pass there is a bar where you can rest after having climbed to 595 masl :)
Beautiful ascent or descent from Ubrique to the Mojón de la Víbora or vice versa, either way we will always have spectacular views of Ubrique and the nearby rocks.
